text.div:
Generated Narrative: ImplementationGuide cms.fhir.us.access

Language: en-US

CMSAccessAPI

The official URL for this implementation guide is: 

https://dsacms.github.io/cmmi-access-model/ImplementationGuide/cms.fhir.us.access

Comprehensive Implementation Guide for all CMS ACCESS Model APIs including Eligibility, Alignment, Unalignment, and Data Reporting

name: CMSAccessAPI
title: CMS ACCESS Model API
description: Comprehensive Implementation Guide for all CMS ACCESS Model APIs including Eligibility, Alignment, Unalignment, and Data Reporting

extension.valueMarkdown: Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on HL7 Terminology
Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on the HL7 Extension Pack

grouping.name: Shared Resources
grouping.description: Resources used across multiple ACCESS APIs

grouping.name: Eligibility API Resources
grouping.description: Resources specific to the ACCESS Eligibility API

grouping.name: Alignment API Resources
grouping.description: Resources specific to the ACCESS Alignment API

grouping.name: Unalignment API Resources
grouping.description: Resources specific to the ACCESS Unalignment API

resource.name: ACCESS Alignment API Capability Statement
resource.description: Describes the expected capabilities of systems implementing the ACCESS Alignment API for aligning patients to ACCESS participants.

resource.name: ACCESS Alignment Request Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the `$align` operation input parameters. When a patient wishes to switch from one ACCESS participant to another after the 3-month lock-in period, the `switchConsentAttestation` parameter must be set to `true` to indicate that the patient has provided consent to switch providers.

resource.name: ACCESS Alignment Request Result Codes
resource.description: This codesystem defines codes indicating the result of a requested alignment.

resource.name: ACCESS Alignment Response Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the `$align` operation output parameters, which provide the results of the alignment request.

resource.name: ACCESS Alignment Result Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Alignment Result code system.

resource.name: ACCESS Behavioral Health (BH) Track Qualifying Diagnoses
resource.description: This value set contains ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes that qualify a patient for the ACCESS Model Behavioral Health (BH) track. Includes depression and anxiety disorder diagnoses.

resource.name: ACCESS BH Condition Profile
resource.description: The ACCESS BH Track Condition Profile is used for representing qualifying diagnoses for the Behavioral Health (BH) track. It constrains the [ACCESS Condition Profile](StructureDefinition-access-condition.html) to add a required binding to [ACCESSBHDiagnosisVS](ValueSet-ACCESSBHDiagnosisVS.html).

resource.name: ACCESS Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) Track Qualifying Diagnoses
resource.description: This value set contains ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes that qualify a patient for the ACCESS Model Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) track. Includes diabetes mellitus (all types), atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD), and chronic kidney disease (CKD stage 3) diagnoses.

resource.name: ACCESS Check Eligibility Request Parameters
resource.description: This profile defines the input parameters for the `$check-eligibility` operation, which is used to determine if a patient is eligible for a specific ACCESS Model track.

resource.name: ACCESS Check Eligibility Response Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the `$check-eligibility` operation output parameters, which provide the results of the eligibility check.

resource.name: ACCESS CKM Track Condition Profile
resource.description: The ACCESS CKM Track Condition Profile is used for representing qualifying diagnoses for the Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) track. It constrains the [ACCESS Condition Profile](StructureDefinition-access-condition.html) to add a required binding to [ACCESSCKMDiagnosisVS](ValueSet-ACCESSCKMDiagnosisVS.html).

resource.name: ACCESS Clinical Exclusion Condition
resource.description: The ACCESS Clinical Exclusion Condition profile is used for representing disqualifying conditions that would prevent a patient from being eligible for the ACCESS Model.

resource.name: ACCESS Condition Profile
resource.description: This profile constrains the US Core Condition Problems and Health Concerns Profile to require ICD-10-CM codes for the ACCESS Model. This ensures consistent diagnosis coding across all ACCESS APIs.  This profile also serves as the foundation for track-specific condition profiles:

resource.name: ACCESS Early Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (eCKM) Track Qualifying Diagnoses
resource.description: This value set contains ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes that qualify a patient for the ACCESS Model Early Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (eCKM) track. Includes hypertension, dyslipidemia, prediabetes, and obesity diagnoses.

resource.name: ACCESS eCKM Track Condition Profile
resource.description: The ACCESS eCKM Track Condition Profile is used for representing qualifying diagnoses for the Early Cardio-Kidney-Metabolic (eCKM) track. It constrains the [ACCESS Condition Profile](StructureDefinition-access-condition.html) to add a required binding to [ACCESSeCKMDiagnosisVS](ValueSet-ACCESSeCKMDiagnosisVS.html).

resource.name: ACCESS Eligibility API Capability Statement
resource.description: Describes the expected capabilities of systems implementing the ACCESS Eligibility API for checking patient eligibility for the ACCESS Model.

resource.name: ACCESS Eligibility Result Codes
resource.description: This codesystem defines codes indicating the result of an eligibility check.

resource.name: ACCESS Eligibility Result Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Eligibility Result code system.

resource.name: ACCESS Event Types
resource.description: This codesystem defines codes for the types of events that trigger subscription notifications in the ACCESS Model.

resource.name: ACCESS Event Types Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Event Types code system.

resource.name: ACCESS Model Tracks
resource.description: This codesystem defines codes for each of the ACCESS Model tracks.

resource.name: ACCESS Model Tracks Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Model Tracks code system.

resource.name: ACCESS MSK Track Condition Profile
resource.description: The ACCESS MSK Track Condition Profile is used for representing qualifying diagnoses for the Musculoskeletal (MSK) track. It constrains the [ACCESS Condition Profile](StructureDefinition-access-condition.html) to add a required binding to [ACCESSMSKDiagnosisVS](ValueSet-ACCESSMSKDiagnosisVS.html).

resource.name: ACCESS Musculoskeletal (MSK) Track Qualifying Diagnoses
resource.description: This value set contains ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes that qualify a patient for the ACCESS Model Musculoskeletal (MSK) track. Includes osteoarthritis, other arthropathies and joint disorders, spine and structural disorders, degenerative spine disease, back pain and radiculopathy, soft tissue/tendon/bursa disorders, and peripheral nerve and plexus disorders.

resource.name: ACCESS Submission Status Response Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the $submission-status operation output parameters (generic for all APIs). When the result indicates a validation error, the optional 'issues' parameter MAY contain an OperationOutcome resource with detailed validation error information to help implementers understand and correct the submission.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment API Capability Statement
resource.description: Describes the expected capabilities of systems implementing the ACCESS Unalignment API for removing patient alignments from ACCESS participants.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Reason Codes
resource.description: This codesystem defines the codes indicating the reason for the requested unalignment.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Reason Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Unalignment Reason code system.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Request Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the `$unalign` operation input parameters.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Request Result Codes
resource.description: This codesystem defines the codes indicating the result of a requested unalignment.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Response Parameters
resource.description: This is the profile for the `$unalign` operation output parameters, which provide the results of the unalignment request.

resource.name: ACCESS Unalignment Result Value Set
resource.description: This value set includes all codes from the ACCESS Unalignment Result code system.

resource.name: Alignment Request
resource.description: The **$align** operation determines if the patient can be aligned to a participant so that the participant can start providing care to the patient under the ACCESS Model. If the patient can be aligned, the patient will be aligned with the participant for a specific ACCESS track.

resource.name: Alignment Request Example
resource.description: Example of parameters for aligning a patient to the ACCESS Model

resource.name: Alignment Request with Switch Consent Example
resource.description: Example of parameters for aligning a patient with switch consent attestation

resource.name: Alignment Response - Aligned Example
resource.description: Example response indicating successful patient alignment

resource.name: Alignment Response - Switch Approved Example
resource.description: Example response indicating successful provider switch

resource.name: Check Eligibility Request
resource.description: The **$check-eligibility** operation allows a participant to submit basic information on a patient who they believe will be a good candidate for the ACCESS model and who wants to be part of the ACCESS Model. The participant can use this API to do a quick check on which patients may be eligible before officially submitting the patient's information for consideration and inclusion in the ACCESS Model.

resource.name: Check Eligibility Request Example
resource.description: Example of parameters for checking patient eligibility for the ACCESS Model

resource.name: Check Eligibility Response - Eligible Example
resource.description: Example response indicating patient is eligible for the ACCESS Model

resource.name: Condition Example - Essential Hypertension
resource.description: Example condition for Essential Hypertension using ACCESS eCKM Track Condition profile with required ICD-10 coding

resource.name: Condition Example - Major Depressive Disorder
resource.description: Example condition for Major Depressive Disorder using ACCESS BH Track Condition profile with required ICD-10 coding

resource.name: Condition Example - Osteoarthritis
resource.description: Example condition for Osteoarthritis using ACCESS MSK Track Condition profile with required ICD-10 coding

resource.name: Condition Example - Type 2 Diabetes
resource.description: Example condition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus using ACCESS CKM Track Condition profile with required ICD-10 coding

resource.name: End Stage Renal Disease Condition Example
resource.description: Active ESRD diagnosis that disqualifies patient from ACCESS Model

resource.name: Organization Example
resource.description: Example organization for ACCESS Model examples

resource.name: Patient Example
resource.description: Example patient for ACCESS Model examples

resource.name: Patient with ESRD - Unalignment Example
resource.description: Patient Jane Smith who has developed ESRD making her ineligible for ACCESS Model

resource.name: Practitioner Example
resource.description: Example practitioner for ACCESS Model examples

resource.name: Submission Status
resource.description: The **$submission-status** operation allows the client to check the status of any asynchronous ACCESS operation submission. This is a shared operation used by all ACCESS APIs. The specific result codes returned depend on which operation created the submission: [ACCESSEligibilityResultVS](ValueSet-ACCESSEligibilityResultVS.html) for $check-eligibility, [ACCESSAlignmentResultVS](ValueSet-ACCESSAlignmentResultVS.html) for $align, and [ACCESSUnalignmentResultVS](ValueSet-ACCESSUnalignmentResultVS.html) for $unalign.

resource.name: Submission Status Response Example
resource.description: Example response for submission status check

resource.name: Unalignment Request
resource.description: The **$unalign** operation supports the ability to manually unalign a patient from the participant in a specific ACCESS track. There are a specific set of reasons a patient can be unaligned such as the patient has moved outside of the care radius of the participant or despite good faith efforts, communication with the patient has ceased. When unalignment is successful, the system automatically sends notifications to the participant confirming the unalignment, then all FHIR subscriptions created during the original alignment are cancelled to prevent future erroneous notifications.

resource.name: Unalignment Request - ESRD Example
resource.description: Example of unalignment request for patient who developed ESRD (no longer clinically eligible)

resource.name: Unalignment Request Example
resource.description: Example of parameters for unaligning a patient from the ACCESS Model

resource.name: Unalignment Response - Unaligned Example
resource.description: Example response indicating successful patient unalignment
